title: linzia glabra var. laxa
intro: Linzia glabra var. laxa, also known as Wormwood or White Sage, is a plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits. The plant contains compounds such as thujone and camphor, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of Linzia glabra var. laxa have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including indigestion, nausea, and skin conditions, as well as to boost the immune system and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or infused oils, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve mood.
